scope_note: Note 1: Chapter 69 applies ONLY to ceramic products that have been FIRED AFTER SHAPING. Note 1(b): products heated to <800°C merely for curing resins, accelerating hydration or removing water/volatiles are NOT considered 'fired' → excluded from Ch.69. Note 1(c): ceramic articles are obtained by firing inorganic, non-metallic materials (clays, siliceous materials, high-melting-point oxides/carbides/nitrides/graphite, sometimes with binders).
inclusions: Heading 69.01 — Bricks, blocks, tiles and other ceramic goods of siliceous fossil meals (e.g., kieselguhr, tripolite or diatomite) or of similar siliceous earths
Heading 69.02 — Refractory bricks, blocks, tiles and similar refractory ceramic constructional goods, other than those of siliceous fossil meals or of similar siliceous earths

Heading 69.03 — Other refractory ceramic goods (e.g., retorts, crucibles, muffles, nozzles, plugs, supports, cupels, tubes, pipes, sheaths and rods), other than those of siliceous fossil meals or of similar siliceous earths
Heading 69.04 — Ceramic building bricks, flooring blocks, support or filler tiles and the like
Heading 69.05 — Roofing tiles, chimney-pots, cowls, chimney liners, architectural ornaments and other constructional goods, of ceramic
Heading 69.06 — Ceramic pipes, conduits, guttering and pipe fittings
Heading 69.07 — Ceramic flags and paving, hearth or wall tiles; ceramic mosaic cubes and the like, whether or not on a backing; finishing ceramics (including unglazed, glazed, porcelain/china)
Heading 69.08 — [Combined with 69.07 in recent WCO versions — glazed ceramic flags, paving, hearth or wall tiles; ceramic mosaic cubes]

Heading 69.09 — Ceramic wares for laboratory, chemical or other technical uses; ceramic troughs, tubs and similar receptacles of a kind used in agriculture; ceramic pots, jars and similar articles of a kind used for conveyance or packing of goods
Heading 69.10 — Ceramic sinks, wash basins, wash basin pedestals, baths, bidets, water closet pans, flushing cisterns, urinals and similar sanitary fixtures
Heading 69.11 — Tableware, kitchenware, other household articles and toilet articles, of porcelain or china
Heading 69.12 — Tableware, kitchenware, other household articles and toilet articles, of ceramic (other than porcelain or china)
Heading 69.13 — Statuettes and other ornamental ceramic articles

exclusions.condition: Ceramic products NOT fired after shaping, OR heated to <800°C only for curing resins/hydration/drying — not truly fired
exclusions.action: Exclude from Ch.69 entirely — Note 1(a)(b). Such products classified by their material composition in other chapters (e.g., unfired clay articles, gypsum articles → Ch.68)
Redirect to heading 28.44 — Note 2(a). Radioactive ceramic elements → Ch.28

exclusions.condition: Millstones, grindstones, grinding wheels and similar articles of ceramic (heading 68.04)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 68.04 — Note 2(b). Ceramic abrasive grinding wheels → 68.04, not Ch.69

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 71 (e.g., imitation jewellery of ceramic, ceramic beads for jewellery not yet assembled)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 71 — Note 2(c). Imitation jewellery articles incorporating ceramic → 71.17. Raw ceramic beads for jewellery → Ch.69 (69.14). Assembled imitation jewellery with ceramic → 71.17
Redirect to heading 81.13 — Note 2(d). Cermets are NOT purely ceramic — they are metal-ceramic composites classified in Ch.81

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 82 (tools with ceramic working parts — e.g., ceramic-tipped cutting tools)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 82 — Note 2(e). Ceramic cutting inserts unmounted → 82.09 (cermet tips) OR remains Ch.69 if pure ceramic. Ceramic knife → Ch.82 if it qualifies (working part of ceramic counts per Ch.82 scope). Tools with ceramic working part → Ch.82

exclusions.condition: Electrical insulators of ceramic (heading 85.46) or fittings of ceramic insulating material (heading 85.47)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 85.46 or 85.47 — Note 2(f). Porcelain power line insulators → 85.46. Ceramic terminal blocks/boards → 85.47
Redirect to heading 90.21 (orthopaedic/prosthetic appliances) — Note 2(g). Dental ceramic crowns, dental porcelain → 90.21

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 94: ceramic furniture (e.g., ceramic garden stools used as furniture), ceramic luminaires, prefabricated buildings
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 94 — Note 2(ij). Ceramic garden stool used as furniture → 94.03. Ceramic luminaires → 94.05. Prefabricated buildings with ceramic components → 94.06

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Mandatory firing test: only fired ceramic enters Ch.69
classification_rules.description: Chapter 69 ONLY covers ceramic products FIRED after shaping. Two critical exclusions from 'fired': (1) Articles heated to <800°C for curing resins, accelerating hydration, or removing water → NOT fired → excluded from Ch.69. (2) The firing must transform the inorganic non-metallic material through a high-temperature sintering/vitrification process. Green (unfired) clay products, air-dried or sun-dried ceramics, resin-bonded ceramic composites → NOT Ch.69. Only after firing verification should Ch.69 be applied.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 69.01–69.03 (refractories/special technical ceramics) vs 69.04–69.14 (general ceramics)
classification_rules.description: Note 1(a): headings 69.04–69.14 apply ONLY to ceramic goods NOT classifiable in 69.01–69.03. So 69.01–69.03 have priority: 69.01 = siliceous fossil meal ceramics (kieselguhr, diatomite — special siliceous raw material). 69.02 = refractory CONSTRUCTIONAL goods (bricks, blocks, tiles for furnace lining, >withstand high temperature). 69.03 = other refractory goods (crucibles, nozzles, tubes, sheaths, retorts — functional refractories). If any of these apply → classify in 69.01–69.03. Only if none applies → move to 69.04–69.14.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 69.04 (building bricks/blocks) vs 69.05 (roofing/architectural ceramics) vs 69.06 (ceramic pipes) vs 69.07 (tiles/paving)
classification_rules.description: Within structural/constructional ceramics (69.04–69.07): 69.04 = BRICKS and building blocks (walling, flooring blocks, filler tiles). 69.05 = ROOFING TILES, chimney pots, architectural ornaments (decorative/functional roof elements). 69.06 = PIPES, conduits, guttering and fittings (for drainage, sewage, conduits). 69.07 = FLAGS and WALL/FLOOR TILES and MOSAICS (flat tiles for floor, wall or mosaic; includes porcelain, glazed, unglazed). The functional use in construction and the physical form (brick vs. tile vs. pipe vs. roof element) determine the heading.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 69.11 (porcelain/china tableware) vs 69.12 (other ceramic tableware) vs 69.10 (sanitary ceramic) vs 69.13 (ornamental ceramic)
classification_rules.description: Four headings for finished ceramic articles: 69.10 = SANITARY FIXTURES of ceramic (sinks, baths, toilets, bidets, cisterns) regardless of ceramic type. 69.11 = TABLE/KITCHEN/HOUSEHOLD/TOILET articles of PORCELAIN OR CHINA specifically (fine ceramic). 69.12 = same use articles of OTHER ceramics (earthenware, stoneware, faience — not porcelain). 69.13 = STATUETTES and ORNAMENTAL ceramic articles (decorative purpose, not functional household use). An ornamental porcelain vase sold for decoration → 69.13. A porcelain dinner set → 69.11. A stoneware mug → 69.12.

Heading 69.09 is specifically for: (a) LABORATORY/CHEMICAL/TECHNICAL ceramic wares (crucibles, evaporators, filtering tubes, labware — excluding 69.03 refractories); (b) AGRICULTURAL ceramic receptacles (troughs, tubs); (c) PACKING/CONVEYANCE ceramic pots and jars. NOT sanitary fixtures (→ 69.10). NOT ornamental (→ 69.13). Residual → 69.14. The technical/agricultural/packing function distinguishes 69.09 from household use (69.11/69.12).

classification_rules.rule: Porcelain/china vs other ceramics: the material distinction for tableware and household articles
classification_rules.description: Headings 69.11 and 69.12 both cover tableware and household articles but differ by CERAMIC TYPE: 69.11 = PORCELAIN OR CHINA only (vitrified, translucent, high-fired, hard-paste or bone china). 69.12 = ALL OTHER ceramics (earthenware/faience — opaque, lower-fired; stoneware — dense but not necessarily translucent; majolica; terracotta). Distinguishing porcelain/china from other ceramics: porcelain is typically translucent in thin section, white, fully vitrified. Earthenware/stoneware → 69.12.
